campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary rule (Ordinance 2326) and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary Ordinance 2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 project
Grant application for pedestrian and bike path improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council introduced the ordinance and decided to skip the usual first reading process to move things along faster. They plan to vote on and adopt the ordinance at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a new ordinance
Council waived the standard first reading to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Specific topic of ordinance not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from several March meetings and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though the specific details about the construction work are incomplete in the provided agenda.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contracts on behalf of the city

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several updates and contracts. Key items included renewing a deal with a company that handles baggage systems at the airport, reviewing policies to keep housing affordable if subsidies end, updating the storm sewer system planning agreement, and moving forward with a FEMA family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system contract renewal
Protecting affordable housing if subsidies expire
Storm sewer system planning updates
FEMA family camp project approval
Review of previous committee meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a planning meeting to review Mountain View's spending plans for the upcoming year, focusing on infrastructure projects and improvements. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the area.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City planning for fiscal year 2026-27

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with a flag salute and highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council used this meeting to bring attention to these community initiatives and the values they represent.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community values and inclusion initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on behalf of clients to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool - city budget visibility for residents
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under municipal code
Community engagement on city spending and finances

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for Santa Clara residents. If you disagree with a City decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from when the decision is made to file a lawsuit challenging it—this is a hard deadline you shouldn't miss.
keyTopics: 90-day lawsuit deadline for challenging City decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for Council meetings
Quasi-adjudicative decisions (permits, zoning, regulatory rulings)
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and a change to how council vacancies are filled. Council members also reviewed requests for new projects to work on and received updates about upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: Changes to federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for housing and community development
Amendment to the process for filling empty council seats
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to officially recognize the building as a historic landmark, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space as an incentive for preserving and improving the structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown Commercial zoning district development
